During the season, they will compete in the [[Super League XXVI]] and the [[2021 Challenge Cup]]. ==Preseason friendlies== On 4 March, Wigan announced a pre-season friendly against [[Salford Red Devils]] ahead of the delay start to the [[Super League XXVI|Super League]].{{Cite web|title=Wigan Warriors confirm pre-season friendly|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-confirm-pre-season-friendly-3154579|access-date=2021-12-15|website=www.wigantoday.net|language=en}} {| class="wikitable" 2021|website=www.instagram.com}}{{cbignore}} |} ==Super League== {{main|Super League XXVI}} ===Regular season=== The start of the [[Super League XXVI|2021 Super League season]], which ordinarily would have started in late January, was delayed to March due to the continuation of the [[COVID-19 pandemic in the United Kingdom|COVID-19 pandemic]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/55619672|title=Super League season faces delayed start|date=11 January 2021}} On 11 February it was confirmed that match week one would commence on Friday 26 March, with Wigan Warriors facing newly promoted local rivals [[Leigh Centurions]] in the second fixture of the round.{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12215059/super-league-2021-round-1-fixtures-confirmed-all-six-matches-live-on-sky-sports|title = Super League 2021: Round 1 fixtures confirmed, all six matches live on Sky Sports}} The full fixture list was announced on 26 February.{{Cite web|url=https://wiganwarriors.com/blog/2021/02/26/2021-fixtures-announced/|title=2021 fixtures
